title: Households by Size: 1960 to Present (row for 2023)
survey: Current Population Survey, Annual Social and Economic Supplement (CPS ASEC)
units: thousands of households (counts); share of households / share of persons (derived)

provenance.source: U.S. Census Bureau, Historical Households Tables, Table HH-4 (Households by Size: 1960 to Present)
provenance.fetch_method: direct HTTPS GET of the published .xls time-series file from www2.census.gov (the Census Data API needs a key and data.census.gov is a JS SPA; the static table file is keyless and authoritative). Parsed with xlrd.
provenance.file_last_saved: 2025-11-18 (per the .xls document metadata; the time series is revised as new CPS ASEC years post)
provenance.note: Counts are the verbatim 2023 row of HH-4 (numbers in thousands). Household-level and person-level shares are derived here (see derived). This is a HOUSEHOLD concept; the PSID gate measures a person-weighted FAMILY-UNIT size, so the anchor decomposition names the household-vs-person weighting and family-unit-vs-household deltas rather than treating this as a level target.

derived.person_level_note: Persons in size-k households = k * (households of size k); the open 7+ bucket uses a size midpoint of 7.5. Total persons in households ~= 318,186 thousand. The person-level share is the concept-matched weighting for the PSID person-level hh_size distribution; the residual after matching weighting is the family-unit-vs-household delta.
